mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive growth in retail sales indicates robust consumer demand and economic health, which directly translates to higher corporate revenues and, consequently, positive S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns do not exhibit a statistically significant positive correlation with RSAFS month-over-month growth over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals a deteriorating labor market and weakening consumer confidence, leading to reduced spending and corporate profits, which drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over the preceding 3 months
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive or flat in more than 50% of instances where the UNRATE trigger condition is met.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of accelerating inflation and declining industrial production signals a stagflationary environment, which pressures the central bank to tighten monetary policy, leading to higher discount rates and negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth > 4% AND INDPRO 3-month annualized growth < 0%
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive during periods where both the inflation and industrial production conditions are simultaneously met.

mechanism_narrative: During an economic recovery, increasing housing starts reflect renewed investment and consumer confidence in the real estate sector, which typically precedes broader economic expansion and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 3-month average is greater than 500 thousand units
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are negative or flat in more than 40% of triggered instances during identified recovery periods.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent expansion in industrial production signifies robust manufacturing activity and overall economic output, which underpins corporate earnings growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a weekly horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns do not show a positive correlation with INDPRO month-over-month growth over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate near the zero lower bound signals an extremely accommodative monetary policy, which reduces borrowing costs, encourages investment, and supports asset valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are negative or flat in more than 50% of periods when the FEDFUNDS rate is below 0.25%.

mechanism_narrative: Strong growth in both payroll employment and retail sales indicates a healthy, consumer-driven economy with rising incomes and spending, which directly boosts corporate revenues and leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month average growth > 0.1% AND RSAFS 3-month average growth > 0.5%
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are negative or flat when both employment and retail sales show strong growth as per the trigger conditions.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in real GDP signals a deteriorating economic environment, indicating reduced aggregate demand and corporate profitability, which leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth is less than 0%
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of periods following a quarter with negative GDP growth.
